metadata.note: Exotic armors often require 'Specialized Training' to use without penalty.

registry.name: Dragon Scale Mail
registry.cultural_connection: Harvested from the remains of a dragon. Wearing this in a dragon's presence is considered a grave insult.
registry.special_skills: Elemental Resistance: Grants resistance to the damage type of the dragon it was made from (Fire, Cold, etc.).
registry.acquisition: Requires a Master Tanner and a Dragon Slaying.

registry.name: Mithral Full Plate
registry.cultural_connection: The 'Silver-Steel' of the Dwarven Deep-Lords. It is as light as silk but harder than iron.
registry.special_skills: Silent Knight: Removes the Stealth Disadvantage usually associated with Heavy Armor.
registry.limitations: Does not provide the 'Damage Reduction' of standard Adamantine.

registry.name: Adamantine Splint
registry.cultural_connection: Forged using ultra-hard meteoritic ore. It is functionally indestructible.
registry.special_skills: Reinforced Frame: Any critical hit against the wearer becomes a normal hit.

registry.name: Spiked Armor
registry.cultural_connection: The signature gear of the Dwarven 'Kuldjargh' (Axe Battleragers). Every inch is covered in serrated blades.
registry.special_skills: Grapple-Thorns: Dealing 1d4 piercing damage to any creature that grapples you or that you grapple.
registry.limitations: Dangerous to allies; cannot be worn in polite social settings (Fine Clothes).

registry.name: Elven Chain
registry.cultural_connection: Glistening links made from star-metal. Elves consider it a gift for 'Friends of the Forest'.
registry.special_skills: Universal Fit: You are considered proficient in this armor even if you have no medium armor training.

registry.name: Mizzium Plate
registry.cultural_connection: Magically treated alloy created by Izzet engineers or Gnomish Artificers.
registry.special_skills: Magical Dampener: When you are subjected to an effect that allows a Strength or Constitution save for half damage, you take no damage on a success.
registry.limitations: Requires monthly 're-tuning' by a professional engineer.

registry.name: Living Armor
registry.cultural_connection: A chitinous, organic suit found in the Fungal Grottos or the Far Realm.
registry.special_skills: Symbiosis: Grants Advantage on Initiative. Can grow 'claws' (1d6 damage) as a bonus action.
registry.curse_logic: Feed the Host: The wielder must expend 1 Hit Die every long rest or the armor reduces their Max HP.

registry.name: Smokepowder Cloak
registry.cultural_connection: Favored by the 'Gutter-Runners' of the city slums. Contains pockets of volatile powder.
registry.special_skills: Vanish: When hit, use a reaction to drop a smoke bomb (10ft radius obscurement) and move 5ft without opportunity attacks.
registry.legal_status: Banned in cities with high fire risk (Elven Forests).
registry.limitations: If the user takes Fire damage, there is a 10% chance the cloak explodes (3d6 dmg).

registry.name: Anti-Mana Robes
registry.cultural_connection: Crude but effective robes stitched with lead and cold-iron threads. Used by 'Mage-Hunters'.
registry.special_skills: Dead-Zone: Resistance to Force damage and Advantage on saves against 'Detect Magic'.
registry.limitations: Heavy weight for casters; reduces movement speed by 5ft.

registry.name: Glamerweave Finery
registry.cultural_connection: Clothing infused with illusion magic. The patterns shift and move like a living painting.
registry.special_skills: Dazzling Presence: +1 bonus to Charisma (Performance and Persuasion) checks.
registry.active_powers: Change Appearance: Can change its color/style as a bonus action (but not its shape).
registry.acquisition: High-end tailors in planar hubs or Elven Forests.
